title: Té Amo — Tea and Art House (sister's project, not Yahir's — preserved in his orbit)
html:
Té Amo — Tea and Art House (sister's project, not Yahir's — preserved in his orbit)

Source: 2023-12-29 Google Doc. Authorship note: based on language ("I want to give jacoby this opportunity but realistically this is gonna be my pursuit") and the female-perspective framing throughout, this doc is likely Yahir's sister's Té Amo business plan — co-shared into Yahir's drive, not authored by him. Preserved here as part of the family-orbit business graph.

TL;DR

  • Concept: Latin-American/Mexican-themed tea + art house in LA (target neighborhoods: Long Beach, Santa Monica, Silver Lake, Los Feliz, Mid Wilshire/La Brea, Hermosa/Redondo, Arts District DTLA).
  • Three-revenue model: tea/coffee + art classes (3×/week, $35-50/ticket) + curated art gallery sales (40-60% commission).
  • Tea menu: 30+ herbs (hierba buena, manzanilla, eucalyptus, mullein, elderberry, cinnamon, peppermint, hibiscus, lemongrass, rosehip, lavender, valerian, licorice root, cardamom, turmeric...), horchata combos (matcha, chai), cacao tea, Yerba Mate (Argentinian, with mate + bombilla).
  • Pricing: hot 12oz / iced 16oz, all take-out; $4.50-6 + tax depending on drink; $15 for 4-serve pot in-house.
  • Food: conchas (sourced from local Mexican bakery), soup specials (lentejas, caldo de pollo, sopa de fideo), avocado toast on sourdough.
  • Art gallery component: contracts for hanging artists (fee/sq inch OR 40-60% commission), monthly themed exhibits + featured-artist nights, live-art demonstrations with tip + commission split.
  • Financial goal: $24,800/month NET — drinks/food $14,400 + art classes $4,800 + art sales $3,600 + extras $2,000.
  • Funding plan: save personal $ + dad investment + eventual repayment; reach out to business owners for help/donations.
  • Marketing: Instagram/TikTok, network at art events, collab with artists, free-tea-for-Yelp-review coupon.
  • Pop-up MVP plan: be present at farmers markets next 2 months → raise funds → market research → low-risk practice run.

Pattern observed

This is family-orbit business documentation, not Yahir's — but it's preserved in his Drive and ingested for the family-business graph (alongside Brotein which is his, his mom's various jobs, the drive thru weed family idea, and the broader idea list). Yahir's family ecosystem runs on entrepreneurial seedlings — multiple people are building/dreaming side ventures.

The doc structure (concept dump → financials → marketing → MVP plan) is the same business-doc skeleton Yahir uses for Brotein and Rankett — possibly a learned template within the family.

The mention of "jacoby" (likely a co-founder, possibly the sister's partner) and the LA setting suggests this is the Valeria-era LA-based sister venture or a different sibling. Disambiguation needed.

The near-duplicate doc next day update is essentially the same content with the pop-up MVP idea moved to the top — suggests the author was actively iterating.

Open questions

  • Who authored this — Valeria? Another sister? A friend? Need to disambiguate.
  • Did Té Amo ever happen? A pop-up? Brick-and-mortar?
  • "jacoby" — partner / boyfriend / co-founder?
  • Why is this in Yahir's Drive — co-edited? Shared for review? Inherited from a family Drive?
